tool.name: Kinfold
tool.description: Simulate stochastic folding kinetics of RNA sequences

param.name: dangle
param.label: How to treat dangling end energies

param.name: time
param.label: Set maximum length of folding trajectory
param.help: The default (500) is very short and meant for testing purposes only.

param.name: grow
param.label: Simulate folding during transcription
param.help: Chain growth event taking place every rate timesteps.

param.name: glen
param.label: Initial chain length for transcription simulation

param.name: fpt
param.label: Compute first passage time
param.help: Stop when a stop-structure is reached.

param.name: met
param.label: Use the Metropolis rule for rate between two neighboring conformations
param.help: Use k=min{1,exp(-dE/RT)}. By default Kinfold uses the symmetric Kawasaki rule k=exp(-dE/2RT).

param.name: cut
param.label: Cutoff energy
param.help: Print only those parts of the trajectory that stay below this energy.

tool.help:
**Kinfold**
Simulates the stochastic folding kinetics of RNA sequences into secondary structures. Folding trajectories are simulated using a Monte Carlo procedure using the formation and dissociation of individual base pairs, and (optionally) the shifting of individual base pairs.
Note that all times are given in internal units that can be translated into real time only by comparison with experiment. Very roughly one time step corresponds to about 1e-7 seconds.
-----
**Input format**
Input consists of an RNA sequence, optionally followed by the initial structure and one or more stop structures in dot-bracket notation.
-----
**Outputs**
- Trajectories: lists structure, energy, and time for each simulation step - A log file containing summary information (options and random seeds) for each trajectory
